disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Disrupted skin (dermatoglyphic) lines, pinpoint black dots (thrombosed capillaries) and pain on LATERAL pinch (not vertical) — plantar wart, not a corn (corns keep skin lines and hurt on direct pressure)
trigger_question_examples: Are the normal skin lines interrupted over the lump, with tiny black dots, and does pinching it sideways hurt more than pressing straight down?
Did it start after using communal showers/pools?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, the skin lines are interrupted with tiny black dots, and pinching it sideways hurts most — and I use pool showers.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No secondary infection (no red/hot/swollen skin, no pus, no red streak) and not diabetic/immunocompromised (screens diabetic-foot / infected foreign body); no atypical/rapidly changing or pigmented irregular lesion (screens plantar melanoma)
trigger_question_examples: Is the area red, hot, swollen or leaking pus, or a red streak up the foot, and do you have diabetes?
Is the lesion changing colour, bleeding on its own, or growing very fast?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No redness/pus/streaking, I'm not diabetic, and it's not changing colour or bleeding on its own.
